content:
Hi! 
To be confirm by @5DN1L, but I think Input section is quite confusing. Could we have something like: 
Lines 1 to 7: An integer [[allowance]] for each day of the week (Monday to Sunday).
Lines 8 to 14: An integer [[expense]] for each day of the week (Monday to Sunday).

Also, in Output, you mentionned "Total Savings", but in your tests ouput "Weekly Savings".

Stub generator
* For simplicity and clarity, please change the first 14 lines to:

loop 7 read allow:int
loop 7 read exp:int

Input
* Please change "values" to "lines" in both descriptions.

* Please use the following as the first description:

Each line contains an integer [[allow]], the daily allowance, listed in order from Monday to Sunday.

* Please rewrite the second description in a similar style.

Output
* Please use the following as the first description:

{{Total Allowance: }}, followed by the total of the daily allowances.

* Please rewrite the second and third descriptions in a similar style.

Constraints
* Please add the lower and upper bounds for [[allow]] and [[exp]], e.g.

0 ≤ [[allow]] ≤ 1000
0 ≤ [[exp]] ≤ 1000

Cases
* Currently, each pair of cases covers a similar scenario, which is good. However, the visible test mostly use larger numbers, while the validators use very small numbers. Please modify Pairs 1, 2 and 4 to use more randomised integers with a wider range of magnitudes (e.g. by using (int)(Math.random() * 1001) in Java).

Thanks.

Hello, I am new to the Codingame community, and this marks my first contribution. I have developed this puzzle as a personal milestone – it is inspired by the first program I successfully executed early in my journey as an IT learner.
 
My goal is to share a beginner-friendly challenge that introduces core programming logic and basic arithmetic, helping those just starting out build a strong foundation. While I recognize there is room for improvement, I am eager to receive feedback and suggestions from the community to refine this work. Thank you for your time and support!
I will gladly take your advice, Thank you!

No problem. Here are my comments on the updated contribution:

Output
* Please restore the line numbers before each description, similar to the Input section.

Cases
* Zero Values (Test 2): Please adjust the case so that the weekly saving is 0, while keeping some allowance values and some expense values at 0.

* Zero Savings (Validator 2): Please adjust the case so that some allowance values and some expense values are 0, while maintaining the weekly saving of 0.

* Negative Savings (Test 4): Please modify some of the input values so they are 1-digit and 2-digit integers.

* Overspent (Validator 4): Please modify some of the input values so they are 1-digit and 2-digit integers.

Thanks.

Hi there! Thanks so much for the feedback on my first puzzle contribution – I really appreciate the guidance as I’m getting started here. I’ve now made all the adjustments you suggested, and everything’s been updated to match the requirements. Let me know if you have any other suggestions or if there’s anything else I should tweak!
